definition: A person who writes news stories
example: She works as a journalist for a newspaper.

definition: A piece of writing in a newspaper or magazine
example: I read an interesting article about AI.

definition: The title of a news story in large letters
example: The headline was shocking.

definition: A film presenting facts about a real subject
example: We watched a documentary about ocean life.

definition: To send out a program on TV or radio
example: The game will be broadcast live tonight.

definition: The people who watch or listen to something
example: The audience loved the show.

definition: Stories that are not real, invented by writers
example: I prefer fiction to non-fiction.

definition: An article giving an opinion about a book, film, etc.
example: The film got a five-star review.

definition: To make a book or article available to the public
example: Her first novel was published last year.

definition: To prepare text or film by correcting and changing it
example: She edited the video before posting it.

definition: To sign up to receive something regularly
example: I subscribe to a monthly magazine.

definition: A person who affects others' opinions on social media
example: She's a popular fashion influencer.

definition: Spreading quickly on the internet
example: The video went viral overnight.

definition: Unfairly favoring one side
example: That news channel is very biased.

definition: Able to be believed; trustworthy
example: Always check if a source is credible.

definition: Giving the wrong idea or impression
example: The headline was misleading.

definition: The reporting of news events
example: The election received worldwide coverage.

definition: A regular article by the same writer in a newspaper
example: She writes a weekly column about food.

definition: A newspaper with short, sensational stories
example: Tabloids often exaggerate the truth.

definition: Newspapers, magazines, and journalists collectively
example: The press covered the event extensively.
